button.name: Button
button.description: Primary interactive element for triggering actions. Use for form submissions, confirmations, and navigation.

loading.description: Shows a loading spinner and prevents interaction

constraints: Button label must be concise — 1–4 words. Use tooltips for additional context.
Destructive buttons must be separated from non-destructive actions by at least 8px.
Loading state must disable interaction and show a spinner.
Icon-only buttons must include an aria-label.
Do not use ghost variant as the sole primary action on a page.
Maximum one primary button per visible viewport region.

accessibility.focusManagement: Must be included in the natural tab order. Focus ring must meet 3:1 contrast ratio.

slots.leftIcon: Optional leading icon (16px for sm/md, 20px for lg)
slots.label: Required text content

anatomy.compositionNotes: Icon size should match text size. Use the Icon component for leftIcon and rightIcon slots.

variantGuidance.secondary: Alternative or less prominent actions alongside a primary.
variantGuidance.outline: Bordered but low-fill — good for secondary actions in white backgrounds.
variantGuidance.ghost: Tertiary actions, toolbars. No background until hover.
variantGuidance.destructive: Irreversible actions (delete, revoke). Separate visually from other actions.

input.name: Input
input.description: Single-line text field for capturing user input. Supports validation states, icons, and prefix/suffix slots.
constraints: Every input must have an associated label — either visible or via aria-label.
Error messages must describe what went wrong and how to fix it.
Do not rely solely on color to communicate validation state.
Passwords must use type='password'.

accessibility.labelAssociation: Label must be linked via htmlFor/id or aria-labelledby.

slots.label: Visible label above the field
slots.trailingIcon: Optional icon or action at field end

anatomy.compositionNotes: Always wrap in a label or connect via htmlFor. Do not nest inputs.

variantGuidance.default: Outlined border on white backgrounds — general forms.
variantGuidance.filled: Light background fill — use on coloured page backgrounds.

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: Small status indicator or label for categorising or highlighting content. Non-interactive.

dot.description: Shows a coloured dot instead of text

constraints: Badges must not be interactive — use a Tag or Chip for interactive labels.
Limit to 2–3 words or a short alphanumeric string.
Do not rely solely on colour to convey meaning — include a label or icon.

accessibility.role: status or none depending on context
ariaAttributes: aria-label when dot-only variant is used

anatomy.compositionNotes: Place inside table cells, list items, or card headers. Do not nest badges.

variantGuidance.warning: Pending review, degraded, or at risk.

card.name: Card
card.description: Contained surface for grouping related content. Cards can be static or interactive (clickable).

interactive.description: Makes the card focusable and clickable

href.description: Converts the card to a link

constraints: Cards should group tightly related content only.
Interactive cards must have a visible focus state.
Do not nest interactive cards inside other interactive cards.
Card titles should use the Heading component, not plain text.

ariaAttributes: aria-label when the card has no visible title
keyboardSupport: Enter / Space to activate if interactive

slots.header: Optional top section (image, icon, badge row)

anatomy.compositionNotes: Use CardHeader, CardBody, CardFooter sub-components for structured layout.

variantGuidance.default: Standard bordered card — use in most contexts.
variantGuidance.outlined: Stronger border, no shadow — tabular lists.
variantGuidance.filled: Subtle background fill — secondary content areas.

modal.name: Modal
modal.description: Overlay dialog for focused tasks or confirmations that interrupt the user workflow.

isDismissible.description: Whether clicking the backdrop closes the modal

constraints: Modals must trap focus while open.
Modals must close on Escape key.
Avoid stacking more than one modal at a time.
Alert modals must not be dismissible via backdrop click.
The modal trigger must regain focus when the modal closes.

ariaAttributes: aria-describedby for modal body if needed
accessibility.focusManagement: First focusable element receives focus on open. Trigger element receives focus on close.

anatomy.compositionNotes: Always include a visible title in ModalHeader. ModalFooter should contain primary and secondary actions.

variantGuidance.alert: Critical confirmations (e.g., delete, destructive actions). Not dismissible by backdrop.

toast.name: Toast
toast.description: Ephemeral notification that appears in a corner of the screen. Used for non-blocking status feedback.

action.description: Optional { label: string, onClick: fn } action button

constraints: Toasts must auto-dismiss (default 5 seconds) unless they contain an action.
Display no more than 3 toasts simultaneously.
Do not use toasts for errors that require user action — use an inline error instead.
Toast messages must be concise (max 80 characters).
Toasts must pause auto-dismiss on hover.

anatomy.compositionNotes: Rendered in a ToastProvider portal at the app root. Do not position manually.

variantGuidance.info: Neutral updates — item saved, process started.
variantGuidance.success: Completed actions — file uploaded, changes saved.
variantGuidance.warning: Soft warnings that don't block the user.
variantGuidance.error: Recoverable errors where context is lost — use inline errors for form failures.

light.name: Light
light.description: Default light theme — white backgrounds with slate text.

dark.name: Dark
dark.description: Dark theme — dark slate backgrounds with light text.

forest.name: Forest
forest.description: Deep nature-inspired theme — dark green backgrounds with warm cream text.

check.name: Check
check.description: Checkmark for confirming selection or successful completion

plus.name: Plus
plus.description: Add or create a new item

chevron-down.name: Chevron Down
chevron-down.description: Expand, open, or indicate downward direction

chevron-right.name: Chevron Right
chevron-right.description: Navigate forward or indicate a nested item

arrow-left.name: Arrow Left
arrow-left.description: Go back or navigate to a previous state

upload.name: Upload
upload.description: Upload a file or send data

leaf.name: Leaf
leaf.description: Nature or eco-friendly indicator — brand icon for this design system

principles.name: Clarity over cleverness
principles.description: Every design decision should make the interface easier to understand, not more visually interesting. If a choice requires explanation, it is probably wrong.
principles.implication: Prefer a plain, legible layout over an elaborate one. When in doubt, remove decoration.

principles.name: Spatial consistency
principles.description: Spacing communicates hierarchy and relationship. Elements that belong together are closer together. Unrelated elements have more space between them. Spacing values always come from the spacing scale — never arbitrary px values.
principles.implication: Use spacing.2 (8px) for tightly related inline elements, spacing.4 (16px) between fields in a form, spacing.8 (32px) between card groups, spacing.16 (64px) between page sections.

principles.name: Semantic color
principles.description: Color carries meaning before it carries aesthetics. The palette is structured so that semantic roles (action, destructive, success, warning) are immediately recognisable and consistent across contexts.
principles.implication: Use color.semantic tokens for UI chrome. Use color.primary tokens only for brand-level surfaces. Never use primitive color tokens (color.neutral.*, color.primary.*) directly on interactive elements.

principles.name: Accessible by default
principles.description: Accessibility is a design constraint, not a post-launch checklist item. Every component spec includes accessibility requirements as first-class data. Meeting WCAG 2.1 AA is the minimum bar; the system is designed to exceed it wherever possible.
principles.implication: Check color contrast with check_contrast before any color pairing is used in production. All interactive components ship with required ARIA attributes already defined in their spec.

principles.name: Tokens are the API
principles.description: Designers and engineers share a single vocabulary: token names. A token name is a promise about intent (color.semantic.error is always the error color regardless of theme or mode). Hard-coded values break this contract.
principles.implication: Reference tokens by path in all component code. When a value is not in the token set, add a token — do not hard-code the value.

principles.name: Composability over completeness
principles.description: It is better to have fewer, well-composed primitives than an exhaustive library of single-purpose components. The design system provides building blocks; product teams compose them into patterns.
principles.implication: Before building a new component, check whether the need can be met by composing two or three existing ones according to the composition patterns guide.

action.description: Primary interactive actions — buttons, links, selected states. Conveys 'this is clickable and important'.

approvedPairings.use: Primary button label on primary button surface

surface.description: Card, panel, and container backgrounds — one level above the page background.

destructive.description: Irreversible or dangerous actions — delete, revoke, permanently disable. Always pair with confirmation.

approvedPairings.use: Destructive button label on error background
Success banner text on tinted background
Warning banner text on tinted background

text.description: Primary body text. Use for any text that must be readable at a glance.

textSubtle.description: Secondary text — captions, helper text, metadata. Never use for primary content.

approvedPairings.use: Caption or helper text on page background

border.description: Dividers, input outlines, card borders. Decorative separation, not informational color.

approvedPairings.use: Input border on page background (UI component contrast, not text contrast)

notes: Never use color.primary.* tokens directly on interactive elements — always map through a semantic role.
Dark mode is handled by theme overrides on the same semantic token names. Code that uses semantic tokens requires no changes to support dark mode.
The brand palette (color.primary.*) may change between design system versions; semantic tokens are stable identifiers.
Avoid combining two saturated colors (e.g. action + destructive) in the same visual cluster — one should always dominate.

hierarchy.use: Hero headlines, marketing headlines. Rarely used in product UI.
constraints: Do not use in forms, tables, or navigation
Maximum one display heading per view
Do not nest h1 inside cards or panels — use h2 for section titles within a page

hierarchy.use: Major section headings within a page.
constraints: Use as the primary heading inside a card or panel
Do not skip heading levels

hierarchy.use: Label headings, form section titles, sidebar section titles.
All body copy. Default text size for product UI.
constraints: Do not set body text smaller than typography.fontSize.sm in any reading context
Do not use for primary content labels or interactive element labels

hierarchy.use: Fine print, legal text, secondary badge labels.
constraints: Must always meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio — xs size is below large text threshold

hierarchy.use: Inline code, token paths, technical values.
constraints: Always use the mono font family for code; never mix sans-serif and monospace in the same token path

scale.intent: The type scale follows a 1.25 major third ratio. Neighbouring steps are visually distinct but harmonically related — xs through sm for supporting text, base for body, lg through 2xl for headings, 3xl and above for display use only.
scale.productUI: In product interfaces, 90% of text should sit between typography.fontSize.sm and typography.fontSize.xl. Reserve 2xl and above for page-level headings only.
scale.marketingUI: In marketing and landing pages, display and 4xl sizes are appropriate for hero content. Use sparingly — maximum two oversized steps per page.

pairings.name: Heading + body
pairings.heading: typography.fontFamily.sans at fontWeight.bold
pairings.use: Standard product UI. All headings and body text use the same sans-serif family — hierarchy comes from size and weight, not font switching.

pairings.name: Heading + code
pairings.heading: typography.fontFamily.sans at fontWeight.semibold
pairings.use: Developer documentation, API reference, token specification views.

lineLength.prose: 65-75 characters (approximately 32-36em at base size). Use max-width: 72ch for all prose containers.
lineLength.formLabels: No max-width restriction — labels wrap naturally with their input.
lineLength.dataTableCells: Constrain cell content with overflow: hidden / text-overflow: ellipsis at a column-level max-width, not a character limit.

compositionPatterns.name: Form layout
compositionPatterns.description: A single-column form with field groups, section headings, and a primary action at the bottom.

slots.section-heading: h2 or h3 with typographic hierarchy matching page context
slots.field: input or select wrapped with label and optional helper text
slots.action-row: Primary button + optional secondary button, right-aligned on desktop, stacked full-width on mobile

compositionPatterns.notes: Never place two primary buttons in the same form. Destructive actions (e.g. Delete account) belong outside the primary form flow, separated by spacing.16 or a visual divider.

compositionPatterns.name: Card grid
compositionPatterns.description: A responsive grid of cards, each representing one entity or item.

slots.card-header: Entity name (h3 or h4), optional badge for status
slots.card-body: One to three lines of supporting text at typography.fontSize.sm

compositionPatterns.notes: Cards should have consistent height within a row. If card content length varies significantly, use a fixed-height card with overflow hidden rather than variable-height cards that break the grid rhythm.

compositionPatterns.name: Dashboard shell
compositionPatterns.description: A full-page application shell with persistent navigation on the left and a scrollable main content area on the right.

spacingConventions.navigationWidth: 240px (desktop), collapsible on tablet, hidden on mobile
spacingConventions.mainContentPadding: spacing.8 (32px) on desktop, spacing.4 (16px) on mobile

slots.nav: Navigation component — vertical, with aria-label='Main navigation'
slots.main: Main content area — landmark role main, scrollable
slots.header: Page title (h1) + optional contextual action buttons
slots.content: One or more card rows, data tables, or widget grids

compositionPatterns.notes: The navigation and main area should be siblings inside a CSS grid or flexbox container. Do not use nested scrolling containers — only the main area scrolls.

compositionPatterns.name: Dialog composition
compositionPatterns.description: A modal dialog for confirmations, forms, or focused interactions that interrupt the primary flow.

slots.title: h2 — concise action phrase ('Delete this item?', 'Edit profile')
slots.body: Explanation text and optional form fields
slots.actions: Right-aligned button group: primary action + Cancel. Destructive dialogs use the destructive button variant for the primary action.

compositionPatterns.notes: Modal dialogs must trap focus. The Cancel button must always be available and must not be styled as a ghost button when the primary action is destructive — use secondary variant to ensure equal visual weight for the safe option.

compositionPatterns.name: Notification pattern
compositionPatterns.description: Short-lived feedback messages using toast, and persistent inline feedback using badge or inline text.

slots.toast-icon: Optional leading icon conveying severity (success / warning / error)
slots.toast-message: One sentence at typography.fontSize.sm. No bullet points.
slots.toast-action: Optional single text link for an undo or view action

compositionPatterns.notes: Toasts are ephemeral — do not use them for errors that require user action. Errors that block progress belong inline, adjacent to the field or action that caused them. Use badge for persistent status, toast for transient confirmation.
